title: MCP tool for upserting prose documentation with diff sanity checks
purpose: Writes prose documentation to JSON files and ensures method drop thresholds are not exceeded during updates.
module_context: Exports the pseudo_upsert_prose tool function and defines input/output interfaces for upsert operations.

steps.content: Acquire a lock for the specified file key.
Wait for any previous operations on this file to complete.
Execute the provided function while holding the lock.
Release the lock after the function completes.
Clean up the lock entry if no other operations are pending.
Validate that the origin is either 'manual' or 'llm'.
Ensure that the file path is provided.
Confirm that the methods array is present.
Normalize the file path to a relative POSIX format.
Escape the file path for use in the prose file name.
Construct the full path to the prose file.
Acquire a file lock for the prose file.
Read the existing prose file if it exists.
Build a mapping of existing method IDs to their definitions.
Process each new method to compute its ID and normalize its properties.
IF an existing file has 4 or more methods and the new payload drops more than 50%, reject the update.
Identify methods from the existing file that are not present in the new payload and preserve them.
Construct the new prose file content with updated metadata and methods.
Write the updated prose file to disk.
Trigger a re-indexing of the source file if possible.
Return the result including file path and counts of written and preserved methods.
